findings.title: Dead field: `price` in RuleSignal signals array is computed but never read
findings.suggested_fix: Remove `"price": round(float(close.iloc[i]), 4)` from the signals dict. Chart.tsx only reads `sig.time`; the `price` field in `RuleSignal.signals` (index.ts line 146) is declared but no code path in Chart.tsx dereferences it. Carrying it bloats the JSON payload and will confuse the next dev who wonders why it exists.

findings.title: Negation ignored in per-rule signal loop: `eval_rule` called instead of `eval_rules`
findings.suggested_fix: The visualization loop at line 441 calls `eval_rule(rule, indicators, i)` directly, which never applies `rule.negated`. A user who negates a rule and enables its eye icon will see markers for the raw (un-negated) firings, the opposite of what the rule actually does in the backtest. Apply negation consistently: `result = eval_rule(rule, indicators, i); fired = (not result) if (rule.negated and i >= 1) else result`.

findings.title: `rule_index` encodes per-side list position, causing color collision between buy and sell rules
findings.suggested_fix: Both buy rule at index 0 and sell rule at index 0 get `rule_index: 0` and therefore the same color in the legend (Chart.tsx line 214, 735). If a user visualizes one buy and one sell rule, the legend shows two entries with identical colors. Use a stable global index: either concatenate `side + '_' + index` as a string key and maintain a per-key color map, or assign `rule_index` as the position in the merged `viz_rules` list so colors are always distinct.

findings.title: Inline legend styles duplicated between marker loop and legend container
findings.suggested_fix: The legend overlay (lines 716-743) is an 18-line inline style object with no name. When the existing `CHART_BG`, `GRID`, `TEXT` palette constants are updated, the legend diverges silently. Extract the legend container style into a named const (alongside the other palette constants at the top of the file) so future palette changes propagate automatically.

residual_risks.description: Marker deduplication on same timestamp: if a rule fires on the same bar as a trade entry/exit, both markers land on the same time value. lightweight-charts v5 may suppress the lower-priority marker silently. No guard exists in the merge loop (backtest.py line 611 area). Low probability for daily intervals, real risk on 1m intraday.

Color palette exhaustion: `RULE_SIGNAL_COLORS` has 8 entries. With more than 8 visualized rules the palette wraps (mod), making two rules visually indistinguishable in the legend. The legend has no text indicator of a collision. This is a UX cliff not a code bug, but worth surfacing before users hit it.

testing_gaps.description: No test covers a negated+visualized rule. The negation-bypass in the signal loop (finding #2) would only be caught by a test that sets `rule.negated = True`, `rule.visualize = True`, and asserts the returned `rule_signals[].signals` matches the negated-rule firings rather than the raw firings.

No test covers the buy-rule-index-0 vs sell-rule-index-0 color collision (finding #3). A test that sends one buy rule and one sell rule both with `visualize: True` and asserts their `rule_index` values are distinct would catch the current duplicate.
